differences.title: DATA STRUCTURES
differences.leftDesc: Supports strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, and more for complex data.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily stores simple key-value pairs with string values.

differences.title: PERSISTENCE
differences.leftDesc: Offers RDB snapshots and AOF logging for data durability.
differences.rightDesc: Data is lost on restart; purely a cache.

differences.title: FEATURES
differences.leftDesc: Includes pub/sub, transactions, Lua scripting, and geospatial indexing.
differences.rightDesc: Focuses on fast key-value retrieval and expiration.

differences.title: MEMORY MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: More sophisticated memory policies and usage monitoring.
differences.rightDesc: Uses a simple Least Recently Used (LRU) eviction policy.

differences.title: USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Can act as a database, cache, message broker, and more.
differences.rightDesc: Ideal for caching database queries or API responses.

differences.title: COMPLEXITY
differences.leftDesc: More features mean a steeper learning curve and more configuration.
differences.rightDesc: Simpler to set up and manage for basic caching needs.

differences.title: PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Due to richer features, may have slightly higher overhead.
differences.rightDesc: Optimized for raw speed and minimal overhead.
